publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-11-16 18:24:32 -0500.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Personal journals, correspondence, and other personal papers.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Douglas Merritt papers, #7744. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Born Sept. 13, 1934, in Lebanon, N.H., he earned a doctorate in English at the University of Wisconsin-Madison in 1964 and served as an assistant professor at the University of Pittsburgh and then at Brooklyn College, where he became a full professor. He retired in 1995. Merritt and his partner, John Payne Hawker, bought a home in Provincetown, Mass. around 1970, where he spent most summers and made frequent trips in the winter. He died Dec. 23, 2004.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Journals removed from 3-ring binders since they were posing a preservation risk. Material that was in each binder was kept within the same folder. Notes on binders made by James were kept.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Europe, end of Madison, Kennedy funeral, "are with John", first Pittsburgh, Detroit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Provincetown, Elliot Brandwein, James Douglas Merritt, Jim Slayman, and Ron DeShon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jim and John in Grammercy Park (Jim died June 30th, 1998)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Last pictures of Jim and John together

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jim's friends Charlotte and John Marsh

did.unittitle: University of Rhode Island diploma (Master of Arts in English)
University of New Hampshire diploma (Bachelor of Arts)
University of Wisconsin diploma (Doctor of Philosophy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mainly typed and and handwritten poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes parts of a biography of H.P. Lovecraft

scopecontent.p: A few other research and writing items.

scopecontent.p: Tape of Frank B. Long, Kenneth Stirling, and James Douglas Merritt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cigar box collaged with University of New Hampshire ephemera with medical bracelet, UNH pendant, and fraternity pin inside.

did.unittitle: Stubs seating plan guide, Billy the Kid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Win, Crawdaddy, Mississippi from within, and Works

scopecontent.p: Includes NYCTALOPS, Fusion, Biobiotechca H.P. Lovecraft, HPL

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inlcludes Rhode Island History, Tamlacht, Revised H.P. Lovecraft bibliography, NYCTALOPS, FagRag, Long Island Review, and Brown Aumni monthly

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inlcudes Spernatural Horrors in Literature, Letters Lovecraftian, TK graphics catalog, Mightnight Fantasies, Rhode Islander, Red Fire Dangers (RFD)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Mightnight Fantasies, Red Fire Dangers (RFD), Coda, Xenophile, and After Dark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Michael's Thing, Trinity Church programs, and a Brooklyn College Telephone directory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Wigstock, A Taste of Honey, Calvery and Trinity Church programs, Expressions of Dread, It Came from the Grog

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Expressions of Dread, Error is and Enlightened State, Dog City, Terminal Hotel, Lapsus Linguae, University of New Hapshire Magazine
